content:
8.2 Surface Sampling
Auger Soil Geochemistry.
 
Auger soil geochemistry was conducted at Emily Wells and also within the Maralinga Downs, Markov and Retreat tenements in 2020. Gyro
Auger Drilling Services of Kalgoorlie conducted the sampling program using a Landcruiser mounted diesel powered auger .
 
All soil samples were dried and sieved to -240 mesh and placed into paper MINSAM bags weighing between 200 - 300 grams. Samples were
analysed by Bureau Veritas Labs in Canningvale, Perth for Au (1 ppb Detection Limit), As (5 ppm), Cu (1 ppm), Pb (1 ppm), Zn (1 ppm), Ni
(5 ppm).
 
Emily Wells (M20/129, M20/128, M20/360)
 
The auger geochemistry at Emily Wells, targeting the Emily Wells shear zone,  strongly suggests the mineralisation in the Oxonia Pit
(discussed in Section 6.1) continues northwards for another 600m. Statistics suggest that 20 ppb Au is anomalous in the data set. Assays up
to 154 ppb Au make up the anomaly. The 50 ppb Au contour is very robust and continuous along strike, as illustrated in Figure 8. All auger
soil assay results are located in Appendix 1.
 
Mafeking, Markov and Retreat (P20/2153, P20/2007).
The Maralinga Downs, Markov and Retreat tenements, located in Figure 1, contain many workings, shallow diggings and shafts and more recent
metal detecting scrapes. Historic production at Maralinga Downs totalled 331 tons at 15.6 g/t Au. Mined quartz veins vary in width from 20cm to 2m.
 
Adjoining the Maralinga Downs workings are the Misery and Markov group. Markov mine records approximately 338 tons of rock were
processed at 18.3 g/t Au. The Maralinga Downs, Misery and Markov line cover a strike length of 190m, illustrated in Figure 9. About 400m
in a south-easterly direction from Maralinga Downs are the Lady Nitouche Group of workings. Historical production totalled 217 tons at 17 g/t Au. The
Two Nations mine produced 10 tons @ 28.2 g/t Au for a contained 9 ounces of gold.
 
The workings, which average 30m deep, are spatially related to narrow quartz reefs striking usually NNE SSE along structural linears within
the Coe tonalite. These structures are evident in the imaged aeromagnetic data. Old gold workings are dotted along these structures, as
illustrated in figure 9.
 
Regional auger soil sampling and mullock dump sampling programs around Antipodean, Warribrook, Maralinga Downs, Lady Nitouche, Retreat and
Union Jack were undertaken in 2020. The 20 ppb Au soil contour, in Figure 9, confirms these structures as geochemically anomalous. Soil
anomalies remain to be tested by drilling. All auger soil assay results are located in Appendix 1.
 
 Historical gold workings.
 
There are over 60 gold and copper mineral occurrences recorded within Fairbairn Goldfield’s tenure, illustrated in Figure 1. During fieldwork
and mapping programs,  Fairbairn collected 99 rock chip samples around the occurrences and has identified many workings that are yet to be
recorded in the WABMines database, 2020. (Inventory of Abandoned Mine Sites in WA Database). For example, several unrecorded old
workings have recently been found near the Warribrook.
 
Victory’s database of multi-element assays from the systematic recording and sampling of mullock around all old workings is in progress.
Rock chip samples weighing between 500 grams and 1 kgm are placed into calico bags and assayed by Bureau Veritas Labs for the same
element range as for the auger soils. Some samples were also analysed for PGE elements and lithium.   Additional workings are expected to
